product: Oracle Fusion Cloud HCM — cancelWorkRelationship worker action
interface: REST API for Oracle Fusion Cloud HCM, resource version 11.13.18.05, REST framework version 4

service.authentication: basic auth in this fixture: Authorization: Basic base64(user:password) on EVERY request

required_headers.REST-Framework-Version: 4 — selects the structured error document; sent on every request

id_resolution.worker: GET /workers?q=PersonNumber=%27<value>%27&fields=PersonId,PersonNumber&limit=2 — the q value is single-quoted with quotes percent-encoded (%27); workersUniqID is NEVER synthesized client-side, it is the last path segment of the item's self link href; zero matches fail locally before any action call
id_resolution.relationship: GET /workers/{workersUniqID}/child/workRelationships?q=LegalEmployerName=%27<value>%27&fields=PeriodOfServiceId,LegalEmployerName,WorkerType&onlyData=true — spaces in the quoted value percent-encoded as %20

action.sync_response: 200 with application/vnd.oracle.adf.actionresult+json body { "returnValue": "true" } (the docs' example serializes the flag as a string)
action.async_response: 202 Accepted (documented as 'accepted for processing, processing not complete') with a Location header naming the canceled work relationship's status URL — this tenant appends a syncToken query parameter that a rebuilt URL loses, so Location is followed VERBATIM
action.async_completion: poll GET on the Location URL; while the cancellation is in flight the row still answers 200; cancellation is complete when the row is gone (404) — cancelWorkRelationship expunges the work relationship
action.poll_bound: polling is bounded by maxStatusPolls (fixture: 5); exhausting it raises an async-timeout carrying the poll count; the action POST itself is never reissued

error_document.rule: every o:errorDetails entry is preserved in order and navigable from the raised exception; the message carries title and the first code; credentials never appear in exception text

map.200: Ok — operation completed, body per media type
map.202: Accepted — accepted for processing, processing not complete

mock_fixture.origin: loopback http://127.0.0.1:{port} standing in for the Fusion pod
